articles.title: Murder Investigation Launched After Woman Found Dead in Rochdale Farm Fire
articles.excerpt: A tragic incident at a farm in Rochdale has resulted in the death of a mother, with a murder arrest made as investigations continue.
articles.content_html:
A mother has tragically lost her life in a fire at a farm in Rochdale, leading to a murder arrest as police investigate the circumstances surrounding the incident. The fire broke out on the morning of July 9, 2026, and emergency services were called to the scene shortly after it began. Despite their efforts, the woman was pronounced dead at the scene.

Following the fire, Greater Manchester Police confirmed that they had made an arrest in connection with the incident. A suspect is currently in custody as detectives work to establish the details leading up to the fire. The police have not released the identity of the arrested individual, nor have they disclosed any further details about the investigation at this time.

Local residents have expressed their shock and sadness over the incident, with many taking to social media to share their condolences. The community is rallying together to support the victim's family during this difficult time. The fire has raised concerns about safety and emergency response in the area, prompting discussions among residents about fire prevention measures.

The investigation is ongoing, and police are appealing for witnesses or anyone with information to come forward. They are particularly interested in speaking to anyone who may have seen or heard anything unusual in the vicinity of the farm prior to the fire. Those with information can contact Greater Manchester Police directly or report anonymously through Crimestoppers.

This incident highlights the importance of community vigilance and the need for effective emergency services. As the investigation unfolds, local authorities are expected to provide updates to keep residents informed about the situation and any safety measures that may be implemented in response to this tragic event.

Residents are encouraged to stay alert and report any suspicious activity in their neighbourhoods. The local council is also expected to hold a meeting to discuss community safety and fire prevention strategies in the wake of this incident. Further details regarding the meeting will be announced in the coming days.

articles.title: Police Officer Injured in Middleton Crash Involving Police Van
articles.excerpt: A Greater Manchester Police officer was taken to hospital following a collision involving a police van in Middleton. Fortunately, his injuries are not life-threatening.
articles.content_html:
A police officer was rushed to hospital after a collision involving a Greater Manchester Police van in Middleton on Friday, July 3. The incident occurred at approximately 1.10pm on Townley Street, where the police van collided with a car. Fortunately, the officer's injuries are not believed to be life-threatening or life-changing, and he is currently undergoing assessment and treatment at the hospital.

No other injuries were reported as a result of the crash, and no arrests have been made in connection with the incident. This collision is part of a concerning trend, as it was one of two similar incidents involving police vehicles on the same day across Greater Manchester.

Later that evening, at around 8.45pm, a British Transport Police van was involved in a separate crash with a blue Kia Picanto on Rochdale Road in Harpurhey. At the time of this incident, officers were transporting a 28-year-old man to police custody. Thankfully, no serious injuries were reported in this case either.

The incidents raise questions about the safety of police operations on the roads, especially when transporting individuals in custody. The Greater Manchester Police have not released further details regarding the circumstances leading up to the Middleton crash, but they are likely to conduct an internal review as part of standard procedure following such incidents.

Local residents may be concerned about the implications of these accidents, particularly regarding road safety and the operational protocols of police vehicles. The police force is expected to provide updates as investigations progress, ensuring transparency and accountability.

Anyone with information about either incident is encouraged to contact Greater Manchester Police. The public can report information via the GMP website or by calling 101. For emergencies, always dial 999. As investigations continue, the community is urged to remain vigilant and report any suspicious activity to help maintain safety in the area.

articles.title: Plans for New Temporary Fire Station in Heywood Approved as Part of Major Redevelopment
articles.excerpt: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service has announced plans for a temporary fire station in Heywood while the existing facility is rebuilt, ensuring uninterrupted emergency services for the community.
articles.content_html:
Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) has moved forward with plans to construct a temporary fire station in Heywood, allowing for the redevelopment of the existing facility. The new temporary structure will be located behind the current station on Middleton Road and is designed to maintain operational emergency response services during the rebuilding process.

The existing fire station, which has served the community for approximately 60 years, is set to be demolished as part of this redevelopment initiative. The temporary station will feature a two-storey modular building with a bay for one fire engine, while an outbuilding will accommodate any additional specialist vehicles. This arrangement aims to ensure that emergency services remain uninterrupted throughout the construction period.

Planning documents indicate that the temporary fire station is strategically positioned just 400 metres from Junction 19 of the M62 motorway, making it an ideal location for emergency response. GMFRS has emphasised that the new facility will be a modern, fit-for-purpose community fire station, equipped to meet both current and future operational needs. It will also provide enhanced facilities for staff training and community engagement.

This redevelopment is part of a broader £38 million Estates Strategy by Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service, which aims to upgrade and modernise several fire stations across the city-region. The strategy is focused on ensuring that fire services can deliver the best possible support to residents, adapting to evolving community needs and operational requirements.

A separate planning application for the demolition of the existing station and the construction of the new community fire station is expected to be submitted shortly. This new facility is anticipated to include a training tower, vehicle parking, and a dedicated training yard, further enhancing the capabilities of GMFRS in the region.

Local residents and stakeholders are encouraged to stay informed about the progress of these plans, as the redevelopment will play a crucial role in maintaining safety and emergency response in Heywood. Updates will be provided through GMFRS and local news outlets as the project develops.

articles.title: Heywood Foster Family Uses Football to Build Bonds with Young People
articles.excerpt: A Heywood couple, Lauren and Danny Sullivan, have shared how their passion for football has fostered a sense of belonging among the children in their care. Since 2012, they have been providing a nurturing environment for young people, using the sport to strengthen relationships and ease transitions into family life.
articles.content_html:
Lauren and Danny Sullivan, a foster family from Heywood, have highlighted the role of football in creating strong connections with the young people they care for. Since beginning their fostering journey with Rochdale Borough Council in 2012, the couple has welcomed five boys aged between 12 and 21 into their home, alongside their nine-year-old son, Daniel.

Football has become an integral part of their daily routine, with the family engaging in coaching, training, and weekend matches. Lauren expressed joy in seeing the smiles on the children's faces as they play together in their garden after school or college.

The Sullivans have found that football not only fosters relationships but also helps children adjust to their new environment. They recalled a memorable Champions League final party held to welcome their first long-term foster child, which helped ease his transition into the family.

Danny, who works as a football coach and scout, has supported one of the 17-year-olds in their care to gain coaching qualifications and become a grassroots coach. This mentorship has created a strong bond between them, allowing for discussions about coaching techniques and personal development.

Another young person, who initially spoke only French, quickly integrated into the family through football. Lauren noted that the shared activity helped break down language barriers, allowing him to connect with the other boys immediately.

The couple hopes to inspire others to consider fostering, emphasising the positive impact that love and support can have on young people in the care system. Councillor Rachel Massey from Rochdale Borough Council praised their efforts, highlighting the significant difference foster carers make in providing stability and a sense of belonging.

Castleton Library is gearing up for a delightful day of activities aimed at young children with two upcoming events on Monday, 13 July. The first event, the Bookstart Bear Club, will take place from 9:45 AM to 10:30 AM, followed by the Bookstart Baby session from 11 AM to 11:30 AM. These events are designed to promote early literacy and a love for reading among infants and toddlers.

The Bookstart Bear Club is tailored for children aged 12 months and older, providing a fun and interactive environment where they can enjoy stories, songs, and rhymes. This session encourages parents and caregivers to engage with their little ones through reading, fostering a positive attitude towards books from an early age.

Following this, the Bookstart Baby event is specifically for babies up to 12 months old. Both events are free, but booking is essential due to limited spaces.

These initiatives are part of the national Bookstart programme, which aims to provide free books to children and encourage reading as a vital part of early childhood development. articles.title: Health Experts Advise Rochdale Residents on Safe Fan Use During Heatwave
articles.excerpt: As temperatures soar, health experts are advising residents in Rochdale on the safe use of electric fans to prevent overheating and electrical hazards.
articles.content_html:
With the UK currently experiencing a significant heatwave, health and safety experts are urging residents in Rochdale to use electric fans safely. This guidance comes from High Speed Training, which has highlighted the importance of taking precautions to avoid overheating and potential electrical faults as many households turn to fans for relief from the heat.

Dr Richard Anderson, Director of Learning at High Speed Training, emphasised that while fans can be effective in cooling down, they must be used correctly to ensure safety. He stated that as temperatures rise, people across the UK will be reaching for their fans to try and keep cool. However, he noted that there are important safety tips that can help keep fans running smoothly and safely.

Residents are advised to register new fans with the manufacturer to receive updates in case of safety recalls. Before using a fan, it is crucial to inspect it for any signs of damage, particularly the plug and cable. Fans that emit buzzing or crackling noises or have warm plugs should be turned off immediately and checked by a qualified electrician.

Cleaning is another essential aspect of fan safety. Dust accumulation on fan blades can strain the motor and increase the risk of overheating. Users should always unplug the fan before cleaning and follow the manufacturer's instructions for reassembly. Additionally, it is recommended to avoid leaving fans running unattended or overnight while sleeping. Instead, residents should cool their bedrooms before bedtime by running the fan with windows open when appropriate.

Proper positioning of fans is also vital. They should be placed on a flat, stable surface and kept clear of curtains or other materials that could obstruct airflow or pose a hazard. Dr Anderson reiterated the importance of these safety measures, stating that taking a few minutes for simple safety checks can help prevent a fire or a nasty accident and keep fans running smoothly.

As the heatwave is expected to continue for at least another week, residents are encouraged to follow these safety tips to ensure their comfort and safety during this hot spell. For further information or to report any safety concerns, residents can contact local health and safety authorities.

articles.title: Plans for New SEND School on Kingsway West Business Park
articles.excerpt: A proposal to convert a vacant warehouse into a school for children with special educational needs and disabilities has been submitted by Whitehall Academy.
articles.content_html:
A new educational facility catering to children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) is being proposed for a vacant warehouse located at Kingsway West Business Park in Rochdale.

The plans, submitted by Whitehall Academy, aim to transform the empty unit into a school accommodating up to 50 pupils. The design includes six classrooms, three sensory rooms, a multi-purpose hall, and various support spaces such as offices and a medical room.

The exterior of the building will remain unchanged, but a secure play area with artificial grass will be created on the west side, replacing previously unused space.

This new school would serve as an extension of Whitehall Academy's existing premises on Whitehall Street, which is a specialist independent primary school focused on supporting vulnerable children with complex needs.

Students at the proposed school may have diagnoses including autism and learning difficulties, and all will have an Education, Health, and Care Plan (EHCP) or be in the assessment process.

The school is expected to operate from 9am to 3pm for pupils, with staff hours extending from 8am to 4pm, potentially including breakfast and after-school clubs that could extend the day until 5pm.

The warehouse was previously occupied by Circuitmaster Designs Ltd, an engineering firm, and has been vacant since October 2024.

articles.content_html:
A trial has commenced at Manchester's Minshull Street Crown Court for Terrence King, who is accused of murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines in Middleton last year. The incident occurred on 22 July 2025, when Simmonds-Caines was struck by King's car after allegedly stealing an e-bike from another boy.

According to the prosecution, led by Michael Hayton KC, King, 60, deliberately drove his vehicle at Simmonds-Caines in an act of revenge. The court was shown CCTV and doorbell camera footage that captured the moments leading up to the tragic event. The footage reportedly shows King driving at speed before veering onto the pavement and colliding with the teenager, who died at the scene from his injuries.

King has admitted to manslaughter, acknowledging that his actions resulted in the death of Simmonds-Caines, but he denies the charge of murder. The prosecution argues that this was not a case of a tragic accident or poor driving, but rather a calculated act of aggression against the boy who had stolen the e-bike.

The incident has drawn significant attention, particularly due to the age of the victim and the circumstances surrounding his death. The footage presented in court was described as distressing, leading some family members of Simmonds-Caines to leave the courtroom in tears.

This case highlights ongoing concerns about youth crime and the consequences of vigilantism. The community in Middleton is grappling with the implications of such incidents, as they raise questions about safety and the appropriate responses to crime.

The trial is expected to continue over the coming weeks, with further evidence and testimonies to be presented. Local residents and those affected by the incident may be following the proceedings closely, as they seek to understand the full context of this tragic event.

Supporters of the initiative argue that the changes are necessary to reduce accidents and enhance safety for pedestrians, particularly children and the elderly. They believe that the measures will encourage more responsible driving and create a safer environment for all road users.

Conversely, some residents have raised concerns about the effectiveness of the scheme. Critics argue that the changes may lead to increased congestion and longer travel times, particularly during peak hours. They fear that the new traffic flow patterns could create confusion among drivers unfamiliar with the area.

The council has stated that the trial will be monitored closely, with feedback from residents being taken into account to assess the scheme's impact. A review is planned after the trial period to determine whether the changes will be made permanent or adjusted based on community feedback.

Local residents are encouraged to share their thoughts on the trial scheme. articles.title: Future of Seven Sisters Tower Blocks in Rochdale Remains Uncertain Amid Tenant Evictions
articles.excerpt: The iconic Seven Sisters tower blocks in Rochdale face an uncertain future as tenants are evicted and discussions about their fate continue.
articles.content_html:
The future of the Seven Sisters tower blocks in Rochdale, known officially as College Bank, remains uncertain as the landlord, Rochdale Boroughwide Housing (RBH), has put all options back on the table regarding their redevelopment. This follows a series of tenant evictions that have left the once-bustling community largely deserted, with many flats now empty.

Tony Warner, a former resident who lived in the Dunkirk Rise block for 19 years, recently shared his experience after receiving an eviction notice last October. He expressed relief at having moved out, citing issues with cold temperatures and high utility bills as significant factors in his decision. Warner described the community spirit at College Bank as having diminished, stating, "You could die in those flats and no one would know you were there."

The history of the Seven Sisters is marked by plans for regeneration. In 2017, RBH proposed demolishing four of the high-rise buildings, which sparked local backlash and campaigns to preserve the community. Although demolition was ruled out in 2023, the recent announcement by RBH to reconsider all options has reignited concerns among residents and campaigners from the 'Save the Seven Sisters' group (ST7S).

Warner noted that the area has changed dramatically, with the absence of local shops forcing residents to travel to town for basic necessities. He reflected on the stark contrast to the past, when the flats were full and vibrant. The ongoing uncertainty about the future of the tower blocks has left many former residents feeling unsettled, as they question the motives behind the evictions and the safety concerns cited by RBH.

As of now, no definitive decisions have been made regarding the future of the Seven Sisters. RBH has stated that discussions are ongoing, and they have not ruled out the possibility of demolition. This leaves many former residents, like Warner, wondering what will happen next and whether the community they once knew will ever return.

For those affected by the evictions or interested in the future of the Seven Sisters, it is advisable to stay informed through local news channels and community meetings. Residents can also reach out to RBH for updates on the situation and any potential redevelopment plans. The ongoing dialogue about the fate of these iconic tower blocks is crucial for the local community, as it reflects broader issues of housing, safety, and community identity in Rochdale.

articles.title: Comprehensive Guide to Taxi Services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport
articles.excerpt: Explore the essential details for booking a taxi from Rochdale to Manchester Airport, including routes, fares, and practical tips.
articles.content_html:
Residents of Rochdale planning to travel to Manchester Airport can now access a comprehensive guide detailing taxi services available for this route. The guide includes information on various routes, estimated fares, and booking options, ensuring that travellers can make informed decisions about their transportation.

The most common taxi routes from Rochdale to Manchester Airport typically take around 30 to 45 minutes, depending on traffic conditions. The A627(M) and M62 are the primary roads used, providing direct access to the airport. Travellers are advised to check real-time traffic updates to avoid delays, especially during peak travel times.

Fares for taxi services from Rochdale to Manchester Airport can vary significantly based on the time of day and the specific taxi company chosen. On average, passengers can expect to pay between £30 and £50 for a standard taxi. It is recommended to compare prices from different providers and consider pre-booking to secure the best rates.

For those looking to book a taxi, several local companies offer online booking services, allowing customers to arrange their travel in advance. It is advisable to confirm the booking details, including the pick-up time and location, to ensure a smooth journey to the airport.

In addition to traditional taxi services, ride-sharing options are also available for those who prefer a more flexible approach. These services can be booked via mobile apps, providing users with the convenience of tracking their ride and payment options directly through their smartphones.

As travel patterns continue to evolve, it is essential for Rochdale residents to stay informed about the best transportation options available. Whether travelling for business or leisure, having reliable taxi services at hand can significantly enhance the overall travel experience. For further information or to book a taxi, residents can visit local taxi company websites or contact them directly for assistance.
